summary: Portfolio remains yellow with persistent critical issues: chief-scout's failing tests continue blocking deployment functionality despite 100 commits, while neutralworking deployment failures still prevent portfolio visibility. Two stalled projects (director, crumble) appear abandoned and ready for archival.

soft-power.one_liner: Maintaining strong momentum with 21 commits this week, still needs CI/CD pipeline

director.one_liner: Remains completely stalled with zero commits for 30+ days, ready for archival

chief-scout.one_liner: 100 commits with active development but critical test suite still failing, blocking core functionality

kb.one_liner: Stable with passing deployments, development pace slowed to 2 commits this week

wellness-session.one_liner: Steady acceleration with 3 commits and working deployments

language-tidbits.one_liner: Peak velocity maintained at 29 commits, needs CI/CD setup for safety

brain-levelup.one_liner: Good acceleration with 4 commits, needs deployment monitoring

crumble.one_liner: Zero activity for 34 days with working CI/CD, appears abandoned

neutralworking.one_liner: Steady development but deployment failure persists, blocking portfolio visibility

head-of-product.one_liner: Strong velocity with 11 commits, has blocking TODOs requiring resolution

cross_project_ranking.reason: High-priority development tool with broken core functionality despite massive activity, blocking team productivity

cross_project_ranking.action: Fix 'deploy to coolify' workflow failure to restore portfolio site deployments

cross_project_ranking.action: Set up CI/CD pipeline to support exceptional development velocity

needs_decision.question: Should this completely stalled game project be formally archived?
needs_decision.recommendation: Archive as cancelled - zero activity for 30+ days indicates project abandonment

needs_decision.question: Should this dormant web-app be archived given 34 days of inactivity?
needs_decision.recommendation: Archive as completed - extended inactivity with working CI/CD suggests natural project completion
